metadata.title: Clifti-GPT benchmark AnnData: reference and query splits for federated single-cell experiments
metadata.description:
Clifti-GPT benchmark AnnData

Prepared reference and query AnnData (.h5ad) files used to reproduce Clifti-GPT experiments across six public single-cell cohorts. Each archive contains the two files required by the experiment registry in the Clifti-GPT repository. Gene expression is filtered to the scGPT vocabulary; highly variable genes (HVG) are not used for subsetting.

Related deposit (init weights, separate record): 10.5281/zenodo.20489646

This deposit provides the minimal benchmark bundle for reproducing Clifti-GPT fine-tuning, annotation, and embedding experiments. Files are split into reference (labeled training) and query (held-out test) AnnData objects per cohort, matching the layout expected under data/scgpt/benchmark/ in the Clifti-GPT codebase.

File

Cohort

Contents inside zip

ms.zip

Multiple Sclerosis

ms/reference_annot.h5ad, ms/query_annot.h5ad

hp5.zip

Human Pancreas

hp5/reference.h5ad, hp5/query.h5ad

lung.zip

Lung-Kim

lung/reference_annot.h5ad, lung/query_annot.h5ad

cl.zip

Cell line

cl/reference.h5ad, cl/query.h5ad

covid.zip

COVID-19 (uncorrected)

covid/reference-raw.h5ad, covid/query-raw.h5ad, covid/Covid.h5ad

myeloid.zip

Myeloid (base)

myeloid/reference_adata.h5ad, myeloid/query_adata.h5ad

metadata.title: HydraRNA: a hybrid architecture based full-length RNA language model
metadata.description:
Here we present the first full-length RNA foundation model, HydraRNA, which is based on a hybrid architecture of bidirectional state space model and multi-head attention mechanism. HydraRNA is pre-trained on a large amount of both protein-coding mRNAs and non-coding RNAs. Despite being pre-trained with the fewest parameters and the least GPU resources, HydraRNA learns better RNA representations and outperforms the existing foundation models on a variety of mRNA-related tasks

Model weights were uploaded here.
references: Li, G., Jiang, F., Zhu, J. et al. HydraRNA: a hybrid architecture based full-length RNA language model. Genome Biol 26, 383 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-025-03853-7

metadata.title: Hierarchical territorial organization of IL1B intron 3 reveals compartmentalized intronic regulatory architectures
metadata.description:
This repository contains all datasets generated and analyzed in support of the manuscript:

"Hierarchical territorial organization of IL1B intron 3 reveals compartmentalized intronic regulatory architectures."

The repository includes:

• IL1B intron 3 FASTA sequences

• Selected territorial microdomains (Z4C2, Z2C4, and Z2C6)

• TFBS analyses generated using FIMO and JASPAR

• RNA-binding protein interaction analyses

• RNAfold outputs

• RNAstructure outputs

• trRosettaRNA structural reconstructions

• Three-dimensional RNA models (*.pdb)

• Supplementary tables

• Manuscript and associated figures

These materials are provided to ensure full reproducibility of the analyses described in the associated manuscript.

metadata.title: Supervised topic modeling via integrated LDA-NMF for discriminative RNA-seq biomarker discovery in glioma
metadata.description:
The identification of latent patterns in RNA-seq datasets plays a relevant role in understanding the underlying biological mechanisms of a disease. When samples come from different disease classes, inferring over these patterns in a supervised approach can unveil potential latent biomarkers of disease heterogeneity and ultimately support improved patient diagnosis and prognosis.

We propose a supervised topic analysis framework that explores the underlying connection between latent Dirichlet allocation (LDA) and non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) to identify latent gene expression patterns (topics) with discriminative power across classes in labeled RNA-seq datasets.

Our algorithm starts with an unsupervised warm-up step using expectation maximization to obtain initial coefficient estimates closer to the expected values given the dataset. It then estimates the model parameters in a supervised framework via Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) implemented in Stan. The model iterates over the NMF parameters, thereby avoiding the LDA sum-to-one constraints during estimation, and consequently converts the factor matrices to a sum-to-one scale for interpretation as LDA topics and mixed memberships. Users may alternatively fit the model directly in the sum-to-one scale.

We applied our framework to glioma RNA-seq data from The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA), after variable filtering via differential gene expression analysis, with the aim of identifying putative biomarkers underlying glioma heterogeneity and their biologically relevant disease-related interactions.

metadata.title: Morphology and multilocus phylogeny illuminate novel taxa in Capnodiaceae (Ascomycota, Dothideomycetes, Capnodiales)
metadata.description: Capnodiaceae is a major family of sooty mold fungi, but its species diversity, host associations, and geographic distribution remain insufficiently documented in China. Many taxa in this family have been described based on limited morphological information or incomplete molecular datasets, resulting in unresolved generic and species boundaries. In this study, newly collected Capnodiaceae material from China was examined using morphological observations, culture characteristics, host and geographic information, and four-locus phylogenetic analyses based on ITS, LSU, tef1-α, and rpb2 sequence data. A total of 37 newly collected strains associated with 25 host plant taxa belonging to 23 genera and 19 families were included. Phylogenetic analyses resolved several well-supported lineages within Capnodiaceae and supported the recognition of one new genus, Hyphopolychaeton, and eight new species in Conidiocarpus, Leptoxyphium, Polychaeton, and Hyphopolychaeton. In addition, 11 new host records and three new records for China are documented. Host and geographic information showed that several taxa, including Conidiocarpus cinnamomi and C. caucasicus, occur on multiple host plants, indicating that host association alone should not be used as a primary criterion for species delimitation. Morphological comparisons showed that the position and structure of the conidiogenous region within the conidioma are informative for generic delimitation when congruent with multilocus phylogenetic evidence. In contrast, minor quantitative differences in conidiomatal or conidial dimensions may represent intraspecific variation when not supported by clear molecular divergence. An expanded Leptoxyphium phylogeny including all available unnamed strains further supports a conservative interpretation of species boundaries in this genus. Overall, this study provides new morphological, ecological, host-associated, geographic, and multilocus molecular data for Chinese Capnodiaceae and strengthens the reference framework for species recognition and generic delimitation in sooty mold fungi.

metadata.title: Soil microbial community composition in two distinct preserved habitats
metadata.description: This study investigates the taxonomic composition, richness and diversity of microbial and metazoan communities in two adjacent ecosystems — a preserved Native Forest and a Highland Grassland — within a well-conserved area of the Atlantic Forest located in the southernmost state of Brazil. Topsoil was analysed using high-throughput sequencing and metabarcoding to assess bacterial, archaeal and eukaryotic communities, divided into microeukaryotes and metazoans, therefore representing four groups of organisms. Bacterial communities were largely composed of Pseudomonadota, Acidobacteriota and Actinomycetota, while archaeal communities were dominated by Thermoproteota. Unicellular eukaryotes showed habitat-specific dominance patterns, with Cercozoa prevailing in Native Forest and Apicomplexa in Highland Grassland; metazoans were represented mainly by Annelida and Nematoda. Alpha diversity analysis revealed differences in richness and in two diversity indices (Shannon and Simpson), which were significantly higher in the Native Forest, with the ordination analyses showing clear spatial structure. Zeta diversity analyses revealed taxon-specific turnover dynamics and indicated that drivers of community structure varied across organism categories. Co-occurrence networks amongst eukaryotes showed well-structured associations within habitats. These results underscore the ecological complexity of even closely-situated habitats and emphasise the importance of long-term conservation of these environments. They also highlight the value of multi-site biodiversity frameworks, such as zeta diversity in capturing fine-scale community differentiation across ecosystems.

metadata.title: Molecular Dialogues and Ecological Outcomes: Deciphering Host-Insect Interactions in Jharkhand's Agro-Forest Ecosystems
metadata.description:
The interaction between host plants and insect pests constitutes a continuous molecular dialogue, an evolutionary arms race
refined over millennia and centered on sophisticated biochemical perception, signaling, and counter-adaptation. Jharkhand, a state characterized by its vast tropical deciduous forests, diverse rain-fed agroecosystems, and rich tribal agricultural traditions, presents a unique and dynamic theatre for these interactions. This chapter provides a comprehensive synthesis of the biochemical and molecular mechanisms that govern these encounters. We delve into the constitutive and inducible defense arsenals of plants, involving secondary metabolites, reactive oxygen species, and complex phytohormonal networks orchestrated by jasmonic acid and salicylic acid. In parallel, we explore the formidable counter-adaptations of insect pests, including multi-enzyme detoxification systems, effector-mediated suppression of host immunity, and the critical role of insect gut microbiomes. The chapter emphasizes the molecular regulation of these processes and integrates modern molecular tools (e.g., RNA-Seq, CRISPR-Cas, nanotechnology-enabled RNAi). With special reference to Jharkhand's key ecosystems and major pests, we discuss the application of this knowledge in developing sustainable, knowledge-intensive Integrated Pest Management (IPM) strategies, advocating for a shift from broad-spectrum chemical intervention to ecological understanding and precision control.
